method_atom: A self-consistent variational mean-field treatment of a coupled bosonic-fermionic system, using a trial state that superposes a coherent (macroscopically occupied) bosonic mode with a BCS-type paired condensate of fermion pairs; minimizing the energy yields the ground-state energy as a function of an external control parameter and of the total number of composite excitations, showing that the control parameter tunes the coherent-mode occupation. The same paired-condensate framework is extended to finite temperature to derive the scaling of the collective-ordering critical temperature with the number of excitations.
object_atom: A semiconductor quantum-dot micropillar microcavity hosting exciton-polaritons — hybridized cavity photons and electron-hole pairs — subjected to a constant magnetic field along the pillar's growth direction.
brief_reason: The coherent-mode-plus-BCS-pairing self-consistent mean-field machinery and its finite-temperature critical-temperature scaling are the transferable method, while the magnetically tuned micropillar cavity with photon-exciton polaritons is the condensed-matter-specific object; the two are cleanly separable.
